title: Can't push image to docker hub
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman pull gcr.io/kubeflow-images-public/katib/v1alpha3/tfevent-metrics-collector

2. podman tag gcr.io/kubeflow-images-public/katib/v1alpha3/tfevent-metrics-collector yeya24/tfevent-metrics-collector

3. podman push yeya24/tfevent-metrics-collector

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Error copying image to the remote destination: Error trying to reuse blob sha256:8823818c474862932702f8a920abea43b2560ddceb910d145be9ba0eb149a643 at destination: Error checking whether a blob sha256:8823818c474862932702f8a920abea43b2560ddceb910d145be9ba0eb149a643 exists in docker.io/yeya24/tfevent-metrics-collector: errors:
denied: requested access to the resource is denied
error parsing HTTP 401 response body: unexpected end of JSON input: ""
```

**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:       1.0.5
Go Version:    go1.11.5
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
rpm -q podman
podman-1.0.5-1.gitf604175.module+el8.0.0+4017+bbba319f.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Maybe related to #4285 ?
Can you include a `podman inspect` of the image you're trying to push?
@yeya24 can you rerun with `podman --log-level=debug push ...`? Are you logged into Docker Hub? `denied: requested access to the resource is denied` seems odd.

Another quick thought, try:
```
podman push docker://docker.io/yeya24/tfevent-metrics-collector
```

> @yeya24 can you rerun with `podman --log-level=debug push ...`? Are you logged into Docker Hub? `denied: requested access to the resource is denied` seems odd.

Yes, I have logged in Dockerhub. 
I think this is weird.  The log-level option doesn't work, or did I missed something? Should I upgrade my podman version?
``` 
podman push  --log-level=debug
flag provided but not defined: -log-level
See 'podman push --help'.
```

> Another quick thought, try:
> 
> ```
> podman push docker://docker.io/yeya24/tfevent-metrics-collector
> ```

Thanks for your reply. But this produced the same of the previous error

> > @yeya24 can you rerun with `podman --log-level=debug push ...`? Are you logged into Docker Hub? `denied: requested access to the resource is denied` seems odd.
> 
> Yes, I have logged in Dockerhub.
> I think this is weird. The log-level option doesn't work, or did I missed something? Should I upgrade my podman version?

The log-level is a main flag and must be specified right after podman and before any subcommand: `podman --log-level=debug push`

Before pasting the logs, please make sure that your credentials are not in the logs. In case, just delete those strings.

Thanks for your help! I have already figured out what's wrong. At first, I mistakenly logged into another account. 

But this push still didn't work.
```
podman  push docker.io/yeya24/tfevent-metrics-collector
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 8823818c4748: 0 B / 113.49 MiB [----------------------------------]

Error copying image to the remote destination: Error writing blob: Patch https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/yeya24/tfevent-metrics-collector/blobs/uploads/899fd789-966f-459e-8dd2-a057b0c42dae?_state=m-OkirdlIIH-pVHeeZTVh3Ui7SDcBR6X7o1s_SeRuq97Ik5hbWUiOiJ5ZXlhMjQvdGZldmVudC1tZXRyaWNzLWNvbGxlY3RvciIsIlVVSUQiOiI4OTlmZDc4OS05NjZmLTQ1OWUtOGRkMi1hMDU3YjBjNDJkYWUiLCJPZmZzZXQiOjAsIlN0YXJ0ZWRBdCI6IjIwMTktMTItMDNUMTY6MTg6MjIuMzI2ODMyOTI4WiJ9: open /home/ec2-user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/8823818c474862932702f8a920abea43b2560ddceb910d145be9ba0eb149a643/merged/bin/bash: no such file or directory
```
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.

@yeya24, sorry for the longer silence. I was busy fighting other fires until Christmas.

Thanks for retrying. If possible, could you share the image your attempting to push. This issue has popped up at other places as well but don't yet have a reproducer.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Closing. Please reopen when there's a reproducer. One thing to check is if there's proxy between Podman and the registry. Some proxy configurations have shown to cause issue.

Happened to me when the proxy allowed access to registry-1.docker.io and auth.docker.io but not to
  production.cloudflare.docker.com
Solution was to configure the proxy to allow the connection to  production.cloudflare.docker.com:443

I have the similar trouble when try to push image to Docker Hub.

My system:

```bash
$ uname -a

Linux fw36 5.17.6-300.fc36.x86_64 #1 SMP PREEMPT Mon May 9 15:47:11 UTC 2022 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

$ podman -v

podman version 4.1.0
```

I start with login to the `docker.io` repository with my account credentials (as always):

```bash
$ sudo podman login docker.io

Username: my_user
Password: ***

Login Succeeded!
```

Then, create Docker image:

```bash
$ podman build -t docker.io/my_user/my_program:latest .

[1/2] STEP 1/8: FROM golang:1.17-alpine AS builder
[1/2] STEP 2/8: WORKDIR /build
--> Using cache e807383a01bea3607e26c2842340ed9b98bd7c03f8f1c3a415b7ccef05bca9f3
--> e807383a01b
...
...
...
[2/2] STEP 4/4: ENTRYPOINT ["/my_program"]
--> Using cache f592b8bcf9b374fa5b14c62df88e02b867527a50fff2d08a2d860574b91213bc
[2/2] COMMIT docker.io/my_user/my_program:latest
--> f592b8bcf9b

Successfully tagged docker.io/my_user/my_program:latest
f592b8bcf9b374fa5b14c62df88e02b867527a50fff2d08a2d860574b91213bc
```

And after that, I try to push image:

```bash
$ podman push docker.io/my_user/my_program:latest

Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 59e46156271f [--------------------------------------] 8.0b / 9.0MiB
Copying blob 1db2971d77f9 [--------------------------------------] 8.0b / 93.4MiB
Copying blob 48f27885bee7 [--------------------------------------] 8.0b / 5.6MiB

Error: writing blob: initiating layer upload to /v2/my_user/my_program/blobs/uploads/ in registry-1.docker.io: errors:
denied: requested access to the resource is denied

unauthorized: authentication required
```

How to solve this trouble? What I do wrong with it? Please help me :cry:
Do you have a proxy setup?
you are logging into docker as root and pushing as a user.

> you are logging into docker as root and pushing as a user.

@baude OMG! That's it. Thanks, you make my day :+1:

I faced the same issue with buildah.  I had to change my  registry to registry-1.docker.io 
`buildah login -u $USERNAME -p $DOCKER registry-1.docker.io`
